mechanism_narrative: Persistent low federal funds rates, indicative of an accommodative monetary policy stance, reduce the cost of capital for corporations and encourage investment, leading to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate, especially from elevated levels, signals a strengthening labor market and improving consumer confidence, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.5 percentage points over the last 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of instances following a triggered UNRATE decline.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth combined with stable, low inflation suggests healthy consumer demand without overheating, creating an ideal environment for corporate profit expansion and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND CPIAUCSL 12-month growth < 3%
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of triggered periods, or the Rank IC is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of extremely low interest rates, any upward movement in the federal funds rate, even if small, can signal a shift towards less accommodative policy, increasing discount rates and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors reprice risk over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.05 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive or flat in more than 60% of triggered instances during a near-zero rate regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing and overall economic activity, which typically boosts corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat when industrial production shows sustained acceleration in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show no significant positive trend (e.g., average return < 0.5%) during periods of consistent PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts, coupled with accelerating GDP growth, signifies a broad-based economic recovery driven by both consumer and investment demand, which strongly supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average > 500 AND GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth > 2%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 45% of triggered instances where both housing starts and GDP growth show a strong rebound.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected acceleration in consumer price inflation, particularly when the economy is perceived as fragile, can trigger fears of monetary tightening or reduced consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns in the short term over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive or flat in more than 65% of triggered instances following periods of accelerating CPIAUCSL.
